skill.name: marketplace-release
skill.description: Guides release creation for Claude Code plugin marketplaces with independent plugin versioning, validation, changelog updates, git tags, and GitHub releases.
skill.summary: Release individual Claude Code marketplace plugins with version bumps, validation steps, tags, and GitHub release guidance.
skill.author: Emasoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, URL, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are markdown release instructions, examples, and configuration paths, not bundled executable code or obfuscated malware. The skill should still carry a medium-risk warning because it guides git pushes, tag creation, file updates, and GitHub release publication.

medium_findings.title: Release Workflow Performs High-Impact External Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run release tooling, validate plugins, create commits and tags, push to remotes, and create GitHub releases. This is legitimate for a release automation skill, but users should review repository state and release notes before approving execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflow explicitly includes git commits, tags, remote pushes, and GitHub releases. No malicious intent is evident, but the operations are high impact.

low_findings.title: Static Command Detections Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Most external-command detections are fenced or inline examples such as gh auth status, python release.py, and claude plugin validate. They are expected documentation for this skill and do not execute by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains markdown usage snippets rather than runnable bundled scripts. The commands are aligned with the release-management purpose.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is a Changelog Example
low_findings.description: The hardcoded GitHub URL appears in a generated release body example for a compare link. No evidence found that the skill sends data to this URL or performs hidden network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is shown inside an example release note block. It is not presented as an endpoint for secret transfer or telemetry.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Path Is Expected Marketplace Configuration
low_findings.description: The hidden path .claude-plugin/marketplace.json and plugin.json locations are documented configuration files for marketplace plugins. No evidence found of unauthorized hidden-file reads or writes outside the release workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path names are configuration locations required by the marketplace format. The surrounding text explains their expected role.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithms at documentation lines, but review found no cryptographic implementation or hash selection. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom cryptographic use in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose or markdown table content. There is no cryptographic code or algorithm choice at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: GitHub Release and Remote Push Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The release process includes pushing commits and tags and creating a GitHub release. These are intended operations but can publish unintended changes if used without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly documents remote publishing steps. The risk is operational rather than malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Repository File Mutation During Version Bumps
dangerous_patterns.description: The release workflow updates marketplace and plugin metadata, script versions, READMEs, and badges. These changes are expected, but they can alter files beyond a single manifest.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation describes several file updates in the repository. This confirms filesystem mutation within the intended release flow.

content.value_statement: Plugin marketplace releases require coordinated version bumps, validation, tags, and release notes. This skill gives agents a repeatable release workflow for individual Claude Code marketplace plugins.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to release one marketplace plugin without bumping every plugin.
Documents patch, minor, and major version bump choices.
Shows validation commands for one plugin or all plugins.
Describes updates to marketplace.json, plugin.json, READMEs, badges, commits, and tags.
Provides generated GitHub release body and changelog examples.
Includes troubleshooting guidance for validation, authentication, and plugin lookup errors.
limitations: Requires external release scripts that are not included in this skill directory.
Requires GitHub CLI authentication before publishing releases.
Assumes the user runs from a valid Claude Code marketplace repository root.
Can publish unintended changes if the repository state is not reviewed first.

use_cases.title: Patch a Single Plugin
use_cases.description: Release a bug fix for one marketplace plugin while leaving other plugin versions unchanged.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature Release
use_cases.description: Validate a plugin, bump a minor version, update marketplace metadata, and draft release notes.

use_cases.title: Standardize Marketplace Releases
use_cases.description: Use one documented workflow for tags, changelogs, GitHub releases, and marketplace README version tables.

prompt_templates.title: List Plugin Versions
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner inventory check before a release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the marketplace-release workflow to list all plugins and their current versions. Do not change files.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Patch Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the marketplace-release workflow to plan a patch release for <plugin-name> with these notes: <release-notes>. Show the commands before running them.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Before Release
prompt_templates.scenario: Check marketplace structure and plugin validity.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the marketplace-release workflow to validate <plugin-name>, check GitHub CLI authentication, and report any blockers before release.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Release Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release with publication risk checks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the marketplace-release workflow for a <patch|minor|major> release of <plugin-name>. Review uncommitted changes, expected file updates, tag name, release notes, and confirmation prompts before publishing.

output_examples.input: List all marketplace plugins before release.
output: Marketplace name and plugin list are reported.
Each plugin shows its current version and source directory.
No files are changed during the listing step.

output_examples.input: Prepare a patch release for a plugin.
output: The agent identifies the current and next version.
Validation and authentication checks are described.
The expected commit, tag, and GitHub release actions are summarized for approval.
The agent maps the error to likely causes such as validation failure, missing marketplace.json, or GitHub authentication.
The response gives concrete checks and recovery steps.
The user is told which release step should be retried.

best_practices: Run listing and validation commands before any version bump.
Review uncommitted changes and generated release notes before confirming publication.
Use plugin-specific tags so independent marketplace versions stay clear.
anti_patterns: Do not run a release from a plugin subdirectory when the workflow expects the marketplace root.
Do not publish while GitHub CLI authentication or plugin validation is failing.
Do not include unrelated uncommitted changes in a release commit without explicit review.

faq.question: Does this skill include the release script?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill contains documentation that expects an external release.py script in the marketplace project.

faq.question: Can one plugin be released without changing others?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow describes independent plugin versioning and plugin-specific release tags.

faq.question: What tools are required?
faq.answer: The workflow expects Python, Git, GitHub CLI, Claude plugin validation, and a valid marketplace repository.

faq.question: Does it update marketplace metadata?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes updating marketplace.json, plugin.json, README badges, and the marketplace version table.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The commands are legitimate, but the workflow can modify files, push commits, create tags, and publish releases.

faq.question: How should users reduce release risk?
faq.answer: Validate first, inspect repository status, review generated changes, and confirm release notes before publishing.
